title: Replication, Multi-Region Access, Backup, and Disaster Recovery · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers replication, multi-region access, backup, and disaster recovery into a new RPO, RTO, replication, failover, and restore dossier. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of RPO, RTO, replication, failover, and restore dossier with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.
Configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
Evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Durability Availability Recovery
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 6613 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate replication, independent backup, restore, and failover evidence; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Durability Availability Recovery.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Durability Availability Recovery, keep Durability Availability Recovery connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. options.text: State the Durability Availability Recovery preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Eleven-nines durability guarantees the application can recover every file immediately. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, run one convenient Durability Availability Recovery happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Disaster Recovery Drill
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-disaster-recovery-drill-1kt9dg" that uses case 1459 to separate replication, independent backup, restore, and failover evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
